feed_role: Lab = generator/feed. The clinic SM pipeline (cockpit) owns GATE-1 dedupe, floor-locked rendering, the single preview, and publishing.
note: One creative reused across Instagram, Facebook & Google Business Profile. 10 posts to choose from, diversified by format/hook/topic, each modelled on a niche over-performer and rewritten in EHN clinician voice. Each post carries a routing block mapping it to the pipeline's pillar→format schedule.

pillar_format_map.conversion · Sun: text-led CTA carousel (booking/assessment link in the caption, not on a card)

palette.note: EHN palette only — never the competitor colours/fonts.

typography.note: NOT Libre Caslon — that is the branded-card lock, benched for this experiment.

render_constraints.rule: Shorten copy, never shrink font. The renderer hard-asserts >=36px and crashes the build on an under-floor size.
render_constraints.card_branding: Cards carry the business name "Elemental Health & Nutrition" (green close line) — NOT the suburb. Suburb keyword lives in the GBP caption only (local SEO).
render_constraints.gbp: Every post ships a GBP hook card, including text-only days (Zernio silently schedules a card-less GBP post — always supply one).

posts.visual_recipe: Solid muted sage-green background with white serif typography. Large headline in italic serif font (18-20pt equivalent): "Why Calcium-D-Glucarate is a Game-Changer for Histamine Intolerance." Subtitle in smaller sans-serif (10-12pt equivalent) below explaining the supplement use case. Minimal design with ample whitespace. "1/7" carousel indicator in top right corner. Handle "@STEVENJUDGENATURPATH" centered at bottom in small text.
posts.angle: How oestrogen dominance fuels histamine intolerance — and why the loop matters in perimenopause

posts.visual_recipe: Close-up talking head shot of a smiling woman in an indoor setting with neutral background, wearing dark clothing and a patterned scarf. A teal rounded rectangle text overlay is positioned in the lower third of the frame with white sans-serif bold text reading "The secret to getting kids to open up" (approximately 10 words). The background is softly blurred with warm wood tones, suggesting a podcast or interview setting.
posts.angle: Walking is a conversation catalyst — movement that opens emotional doors, not just physical ones.
posts.on_image:
Hook frame (close-up talking head, warm smile, neutral background): "The magic happens in the *walking*, not the destination"

[Optional 2-3 beat script outline if this runs as a short reel:
Beat 1: Clinician on camera, warm introduction — "Walking isn't just exercise. It's where real conversations happen."
Beat 2: Brief pause/soft transition — visual of calm outdoor step or muted movement.
Beat 3: Close with validation — "When we move together, we open up together. Try it today."]
posts.build_brief:
Format: Reel/short-form video (9:16, vertical).

Opening hook frame (0–2 sec): Close-up talking head of an EHN clinician (or relatable practitioner) — warm smile, calm eye contact, indoor or softly blurred natural setting. Neutral warm-toned background (soft beige, pale cream, or muted charcoal). Wearing calm, warm-toned, professional clothing — no patterns necessary, focus on genuine warmth.

Text overlay (lower third, 1–2 sec in): A rounded rectangle shape in EHN green #39B54A (not teal) with white sans-serif bold text (Satoshi bold, ~95–110px on 1080 canvas, headline hierarchy). Text reads: "The magic happens in the *walking*, not the destination" — ONE meaningful word (*walking* or *magic*) can be italicised in white for emphasis, or leave all white for clean readability.

Whitespace & composition: Mirror the winner's lower-third overlay placement and breathing room. Keep the frame 60% face/warmth, 40% soft negative space. The green rounded rectangle should feel spacious, not cramped — adequate padding inside the shape.

Colour palette: EHN green #39B54A as the primary visual anchor (the text box). Background tones: warm cream, soft beige, or pale neutral grey — nothing dark or clashing. If the clinician wears clothing, choose warm, grounded tones (sage, cream, warm grey, soft terracotta) that harmonize with the green, never compete with it.

Typography: Satoshi bold for the headline (white text on green background), ~95–110px. No body copy on this frame — the video script carries the voice. Keep the overlay text short and punchy (max ~8 words).

Legibility lock: headline text >= 36px (enforce ~95–110px), white on green for maximum contrast. Background and clinician should be soft, warm, and non-distracting.

Logo & close: Small EHN logo (unobtrusive, bottom corner or as part of the final frame if video extends). Call to action embedded in the reel description or final frame: "Book a conversation. Link in bio."

Brand feel: Clinical, warm, conversational — a trusted practitioner reflecting genuine connection. Editorial and grounded, never stock-wellness.

posts.visual_recipe: Portrait of a middle-aged man wearing glasses, positioned center-frame holding a supplement bottle toward camera (proof/credibility prop), shot in warm-lit home office with blurred shelving backdrop. Bold white sans-serif headline (all-caps, ~40-50pt) at top reads "HOW I REVERSED MY BIOLOGICAL AGE", with the specific claim "FROM 52 TO 42" in bright red (~60pt, maximum contrast). Dark teal/charcoal gradient background overlay maintains legibility. Authoritative, "expert in their own lab" composition emphasizing the messenger and the concrete result."
posts.angle: Why functional medicine clinics test methylation markers — and what a borderline MTHFR result really means for energy, mood and long-term health.
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: single portrait-orientation card (1080 × 1350px). 

Background: soft cream (#F5F1ED) with a subtle sage-grey gradient overlay (top-to-bottom, very soft, not dramatic) to maintain a clinical warmth without competing with the green anchor.

Composition: centre-frame portrait photograph of a functional-medicine clinician or practitioner (professional headshot, warm home-office setting, natural light, wearing lab coat or business casual, holding a pathology report or reference card toward camera as a credibility anchor — not a supplement bottle, a clinical document). Blurred shelving or cabinet backdrop suggests clinical authority without distraction.

Headline placement (top third, centre-aligned): 
- Main headline in Satoshi Bold, ~95–100px, dark charcoal (#2A2A2A), all-caps: "WHY YOUR MTHFR"
- Second line, same style, introduces the green accent: "RESULT MATTERS" with the word "MATTERS" in Satoshi Bold italic, EHN green #39B54A (~95–100px).
- Below headline, a supporting subline in Satoshi Medium, ~48px, charcoal: "Even when it sits 'in range'."

Accent bar: a vertical accent stripe running along the left edge of the card in EHN green #39B54A (~40px wide), top-to-bottom, anchoring the entire composition.

Body copy zone (centre, beneath headline):
- A short, credibility-building statement in Satoshi Medium, ~42px, charcoal, left-aligned:
  "Functional pathology reveals what standard ranges miss."
- Below: a three-line supporting note, Satoshi Medium, ~40px, charcoal:
  "Methylation capacity affects energy.
  Mood. Detoxification.
  One marker is just the start."

Lower third (footer):
- Horizontal divider line in EHN green #39B54A (~2px).
- Brand lockup: small EHN logo + "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium, ~36px, charcoal, centre-aligned.
- Soft CTA button or text: "Learn more" in white text on a subtle EHN green #39B54A rounded rectangle (no harsh borders, ~44px tall, ~200px wide), centred above the footer divider.

Overall feel: authoritative, clinical, warm — a functional-medicine practitioner speaking directly to someone who suspects their metabolism is off. The green accent stripe anchors the colour world while the soft cream-and-sage palette keeps the mood grounded and professional, never clinical-cold.

posts.visual_recipe: Split-screen video format with bold white text-on-black banner at top reading "How to break a panic attack in 4 steps" (sans-serif, ~18-24pt, centre-aligned), positioned above a video still showing a credible male authority figure (doctor/practitioner in white coat with red tie) seated in professional office setting with bookshelves and plants visible. Clean, clinical aesthetic with high contrast between text overlay and background.
posts.angle: 4-step somatic reset for panic: grounding tools that work, clinician-verified
posts.build_brief:
Canvas: vertical reel/video format (1080×1920px).

Layout structure (replicating winner's split-screen hierarchy): 
- TOP BANNER (occupying ~15% of frame): Bold headline in Satoshi Bold (~95px+) reading "How to calm a panic response in 4 steps" — centre-aligned, white/cream text on a soft sage-green background (a muted complement to #39B54A, not the main green itself). The word "calm" sits in EHN green #39B54A italic or small accent green underline for visual anchor.
- MIDDLE SECTION (occupying ~60% of frame): Warm editorial-style still or headshot — a calm, credible female practitioner or clinician in a soft neutral office setting (natural light, subtle greenery, warm wood furniture, no harsh hospital aesthetic). Professional but approachable; the background should harmonise with the sage-green banner palette.
- BOTTOM TEXT OVERLAY (occupying ~25% of frame): A clean, minimal charcoal or deep-neutral text block on white or cream background, listing the 4 steps in short-form bulleted or numbered format:
  1. Ground your feet (5 senses)
  2. Slow your breath (4-7-8 count)
  3. Name what you feel (nervous system language)
  4. Move gently (shake, sway, walk)
Each line ~40–50px (body hierarchy), left-aligned, ample whitespace between.
- CLOSE LINE (bottom corner, small, unobtrusive): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in charcoal, Satoshi Medium.

Overall feel: Clinical but warm, editorial authority without hospital coldness. Sage-green + cream + charcoal palette, with EHN green #39B54A as the anchor highlight. High contrast, clean hierarchy, spacious composition — matches the winner's split-screen clarity but dressed in EHN's functional-medicine aesthetic.

posts.visual_recipe: Newspaper front-page screenshot (The Times Saturday) featuring a professional headshot of a male doctor in white shirt with stethoscope on right side. Large headline "Drug-free doctor: The GP who..." in serif font spans left to right. Body text in columns on left, with a pull-quote in larger text: "The number of prescriptions we give out as doctors has increased by over 80 per cent in the past 20 years." White/cream background with black serif typography, blue-tinted accent box around the doctor's photo. Highly credible, newspaper-as-proof aesthetic.
posts.angle: Fasting in modern medicine: why functional practitioners are rethinking the constant-meal narrative.

posts.visual_recipe: Text-only Facebook post with no image. Heavy contrarian hook ("Your vet probably isn't telling you this...") leading into fear-based stat-drops (3 in 4 dogs, lifespan decline) and authority credentialing (40-year vet, 9 books). Four bulleted claims in bold relief, each building urgency around hidden harms (rendered animals, neurotoxins, unnecessary procedures). Closes with CTA to podcast episode. Design relies on caption hierarchy, line breaks, and emoji (👉) for visual chunking in feed."
posts.angle: Functional-medicine reality check: how indoor air toxins (mould, microplastics, VOCs) silently drive chronic inflammation — and why most GPs don't screen for them.
posts.build_brief:
TEXT-ONLY FACEBOOK POST (no image, no carousel). Format: single-column post card, white or cream background (#F9F7F4 or #FFFFFF), with deliberate whitespace and line breaks for feed readability.

LAYOUT (faithful to winner structure):
- Canvas: 1080px square (vertical, text-heavy, minimal visual distraction)
- Background: Cream (#F9F7F4) or soft white, no border
- Text hierarchy in Satoshi: Headline bold (700), ~95–110px, ONE word in EHN green #39B54A italic or emphasis (e.g., "*toxins*" or "*hidden*"); body copy in Satoshi medium (500), ~48–52px for short contrarian hook line, then ~40–44px for stat/claim lines
- Opening hook (contrarian, fear-register): "Most GPs never test for this..." or "Your home might be making you sick. Here's why..." — set in bold, large, lead with the pattern-interrupt
- Stat/credibility drop: 2–3 shocking claims in rapid succession (whitespace between), each 40–44px, building urgency. Examples: "1 in 3 homes test positive for mycotoxins. Symptoms often labelled 'anxiety' or 'fatigue'." or "Microplastics found in lung tissue. Chronic inflammation quietly compounds."
- Bulleted claims (4 claims, each bold and short): Use bullet or emoji anchor (👉) at left margin, ~44px bold Satoshi, each claim a single short phrase (e.g., "👉 Mould spores trigger mast-cell activation" / "👉 VOCs cross the blood-brain barrier" / "👉 Most people never get tested" / "👉 Functional testing changes the diagnosis"). Whitespace between bullets for visual relief.
- CTA line (soft, clinician-warm): "We screen for environmental toxins as part of root-cause assessment. Book a consult to explore your home and health history." — ~40px Satoshi medium, charcoal (#2C2C2C)
- Closing brand line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, Satoshi bold, ~36px, bottom-right or centre
- Colour accents: EHN green #39B54A ONLY as the emphasis word/brand close; secondary palette (sage #A8B5A0, warm grey #7A7A7A, or soft charcoal #3E3E3E) for subtext and bullets to maintain harmony
- Feel: Clinical, credible, editorial — no emojis except the directional 👉 for structure. Plenty of air, no clutter. Font sizes strictly enforced (no sub-40px body text, no sub-36px captions).

CRITICAL: This is a text-only design. Visual anchor = green #39B54A in headline emphasis + green brand close line. The whitespace and Satoshi hierarchy do the work.

routing.gate1_note: Topic "toxins/mould/microplastics" ran in EHN's last ~4 weeks — pipeline GATE-1 will likely collapse/drop this pick.

posts.visual_recipe: Clean, minimal layout with a small circular headshot (beige/tan background) of a professional man in the top left, followed by his name "Mark Hyman, M.D." and verified handle in medium-weight sans-serif. Main body text in large, black, ultra-light sans-serif font on white background, arranged in five short lines (listicle-style statements) followed by a bold closing line. No photographic background, no overlays—pure typography-driven authority play with the credible face acting as social proof anchor.
posts.angle: Nervous-system rest isn't a luxury—it's essential biochemistry for mental clarity and mood resilience.
posts.build_brief: Canvas: single square card (1080 × 1080px). Background: soft cream (#F5F3F0 or similar warm neutral that complements EHN green). Top left: small circular headshot (120–140px diameter, soft sage or warm-grey border, no harsh frame) of an EHN clinician or founder—position on the left side with 40px left margin, 60px top margin. Immediately to the right and below the circle, in Satoshi Medium (charcoal, ~24px): name, title, and credentials (e.g., "Sarah Chen, Naturopath" or "Jane Smith, M.Clin.Nutr."), then verified handle in a lighter grey (~18px, no emoji required). Main body: centre-left composition, starting ~280px from top. Headline in Satoshi Bold (~95–100px, black or charcoal) reads in 5 short lines (listicle-style statements—one phrase per line, max 8 words each). Within the headline, ONE meaningful word (e.g., "rest", "parasympathetic", "cortisol") appears in EHN green #39B54A italic, bold weight. Below headline, light grey Satoshi Medium (~44–48px, leading +12–15px) delivers a 2–3 sentence closing thought on why this matters. Bottom right corner (or bottom line): "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Medium (18–20px), with the business name in green #39B54A. No photographic background, no overlays. Pure typography hierarchy on cream, anchored by the clinician's face and green accent word. Abundant whitespace—80–100px margins top/bottom, 60–80px sides. The green word and the face create dual trust anchors.

posts.visual_recipe: Close-up headshot of a fit, grey-haired man in formal black suit and grey tie, smiling directly at camera in what appears to be an official/governmental setting with blurred background figures and tan leather seating. Large white serif text overlaid in left-aligned blocks reads "I just turned 66." on top, followed by "Here are 5 things I wish I knew at 26..." below, creating a listicle teaser format against the dark suit contrast.
posts.angle: What I'd tell my younger self about building a resilient body: five functional-medicine lessons from 20+ years of clinical practice.
posts.build_brief:
Format: carousel, 5 slides.

**Slide 1 (hero/hook):** 
Start with a portrait-style headshot of a practitioner or trusted clinician face — warm, directly to camera, professional-clinical setting (soft office background, blurred but credible). Wear neutral/muted tones (sage, charcoal, cream).

**Canvas & background:** 1080×1350 px vertical card. Soft cream or warm-white background (not pure white, never the competitor's tan/dark palette). No full-bleed photo — let the portrait sit left-aligned or center with breathing room.

**Primary visual anchor:** EHN green #39B54A. The green appears as:
- A vertical accent bar (20–30px wide) running down the left edge or as a subtle frame/border element around the portrait.
- The headline text (one word) in green italic bold.

**Typography on Slide 1:**
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~95px+, left-aligned or center-left): "I wish" or "Looking back" — ONE meaningful word in green #39B54A italic.
- Subline (Satoshi Medium, ~50–60px, relative hierarchy): "I'd tell my younger self..." (SHORT, max 8–10 words).
- Supporting line (Satoshi Medium, ~40px, charcoal): "Five lessons from 20+ years in functional medicine" (readable, warm tone, no jargon).

**Slide 1 text placement:** Left-aligned text blocks with generous whitespace (mimic the winner's left-aligned serif blocks, but render in Satoshi bold + medium, green-anchored). Text sits on the cream/white background; portrait is the visual anchor (hero image on same canvas, not separate).

**Slides 2–5 (list items):**
Each slide introduces ONE lesson. Format:
- Background: same cream/white (consistent with Slide 1).
- Green accent: a small circle, line, or shape next to each lesson number (e.g., green bullet, green number 1–5 in italic bold).
- Headline (Satoshi Bold, ~70–80px): "Lesson 1: Sleep is not negotiable" (short, punchy, one word in green or italic).
- Body copy (Satoshi Medium, ~45–50px): A single short sentence or phrase explaining the lesson in plain English, clinical tone. Max 2–3 lines.

**Colour palette:**
- EHN green #39B54A (headline accent, bullets, accent bars, borders).
- Secondary: sage green or soft teal as a complementary accent (optional, for subtle depth — never dominant).
- Background: warm cream, off-white, or soft charcoal (NOT pure black, NOT the competitor's exact tan/brown).
- Text: charcoal or deep neutral (Satoshi Medium), green for accents only.

**Whitespace & density:** Match the winner's open, breathing layout — generous margins, clear hierarchy, no crowding. Each slide feels editorial and intentional.

**Branding:** Small EHN logo (unobtrusive, bottom-right corner) on Slide 1 or Slide 5. "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green or charcoal at the base of the final slide (or as a consistent footer in the carousel).

**Legibility lock:** All text >=36px (body >=40px, headline ~95px+ on main slide). NO sub-36px text. If copy is long, trim it — never shrink the font.

posts.visual_recipe: Bathroom shower scene with person in dark t-shirt and shorts standing in dimly lit space; soft warm orange/amber ambient lighting from candles on shower floor creating moody, intimate atmosphere; white serif bold headline text overlaid in center reading "You were today years old when you found out you should be taking dark showers every night. Here's why.." - text has dark semi-transparent backing for readability. Cool-toned bathroom tiles and glass shower door frame the warm golden lighting, creating high contrast between clinical space and therapeutic ambiance.
posts.angle: Evening light exposure is sabotaging your nervous system — here's why dimming matters for sleep and stress.
posts.build_brief:
Format: vertical reel (9:16) or short-form video cover card (1080 × 1350px square option).

Scene composition (EXACT layout match to winner):
- Primary visual: intimate, dimly lit bathroom or bedroom scene; person in comfortable everyday clothes (neutral palette: cream, soft grey, charcoal) standing or sitting in a softly lit space
- Lighting anchor: warm, gentle light source (soft lamp, bedside glow, or cream/sage ambient tones — NO orange/amber competitor palette) creating a calming, introspective mood. Secondary accent: soft sage or teal-grey tones in the background (tiles, wall, or soft furnishing) to anchor EHN green harmony without copying the clinical contrast
- Background: muted cream, soft charcoal, or sage-grey — restful, not stark. Avoid pure black; maintain warmth and clinical-but-tender feel
- Text overlay placement (MATCHING WINNER'S COMPOSITION): bold headline in center-overlay with semi-transparent backing (charcoal or sage-tinted, not black) for readability. Text scale: headline ~95–110px relative size (Satoshi Bold), subline ~50–60px relative (Satoshi Medium). Short, punchy copy so nothing renders below 36px

Headline treatment:
- Main headline: "You were today years old when you found out [SLEEP ANGLE]" OR similar personal-story hook in Satoshi Bold, large and centered
- ONE meaningful word (e.g., "light", "sleep", "stress") highlighted in EHN green #39B54A italic or a complementary teal-sage accent
- Subline (supporting detail): smaller, Satoshi Medium in charcoal or neutral, placed below headline. Short sentence anchoring the functional-medicine angle

Colour palette (green-anchored, harmonises with warm/cool mood):
- Primary anchor: EHN green #39B54A — appears in the highlighted word, possibly a thin accent line or glow, NOT as a full background
- Secondary palette: cream (60%), soft sage or muted teal-grey (25%), charcoal (15%). These complement green without the competitor's orange warmth
- Text: Satoshi Bold (headline in charcoal or charcoal-green layered), Satoshi Medium (body in neutral charcoal)
- Lighting: warm but muted (cream/butter tones in the ambient, not orange fire)

Composition flow (MATCHING WINNER'S STRUCTURE):
- Top 1/3: breathing room (white/cream space)
- Middle 1/2: headline + one highlighted word in green, semi-transparent backing for contrast
- Lower 1/3: subline + a soft visual anchor (small sage/teal accent shape or subtle icon — e.g., a moon, a gentle wave, a soft circle in sage or teal)
- Bottom: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in green #39B54A, small and unobtrusive

Feel: clinical-warm, introspective, hopeful (matching winner's "hope" register). Moody but not dark or alarming. Like stepping into a trusted functional-medicine space at dusk.

posts.visual_recipe: Minimalist text-only card on solid off-white/cream background. Opens with bold uppercase headline (~80 words) framing a wellness philosophy, followed by a bulleted list of 11 micro-practices in lighter weight sans-serif. Closes with a short paragraph and attribution line. All copy is left-aligned in black serif or clean sans font. High whitespace, clean typography, no images or faces—designed for readability and scrolling impact.
posts.angle: How the nervous system talks to your hormones—and why perimenopause feels like a body betrayal (it's not).
posts.build_brief:
Create a square card (1080 × 1080px) on a warm cream background (#F5F1ED or similar soft neutral). 

Layout: Minimalist, text-only, high whitespace. Left-align all copy in a clean hierarchy.

Headline placement: Top third, in Satoshi Bold 700. Use ONE meaningful word ("nervous" or "talk") in green #39B54A italic as the visual anchor; the rest in charcoal (#2C2C2C). Headline is ~8 words max, around 95–110px relative scale to fill the upper space without crowding.

Body section: Below headline, a short introductory line in Satoshi Medium 500, charcoal, ~50–55px relative scale explaining the HPA axis in plain language (2–3 lines max). Then a bulleted list (11–12 micro-practices or clinical insights) in Satoshi Medium 500, charcoal, ~40–45px relative scale, each bullet a short phrase (5–7 words). Use soft sage green (#8BA896 or #9DBB9D) or a warm taupe accent for bullet points to complement the primary green without competing.

Closing: Below the list, a short closing paragraph (2–3 sentences) in Satoshi Medium 500, charcoal, ~45px relative scale, reframing perimenopause as a signal, not a failure. 

Bottom close line: "Elemental Health & Nutrition" in Satoshi Bold 700, green #39B54A, ~40px relative scale, left-aligned.

Whitespace: generous margins (60–80px on sides, 80–100px top/bottom). Let the cream breathe. No borders, no images, no faces. The green word in the headline is the only colour accent—the design leans heavily on typography and whitespace for impact.

This is a clinical, warm, editorial card that feels intentional and calm. Never pure black, never the competitor's blue or exact palette.
